decisions: Host validation placed before ALL routing (including the 404 branch) — sole way to guarantee AC-2's renderer-never-reached and keep 404 reachable only by allowed hosts.
Fail-closed parsing: every malformed Host value rejects — security hardening must not guess intent; matches brief line 10.

Literal allowlist comparison, no canonicalization — [0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1], 127.0.0.2, localhost. all reject; browsers send the literal forms, and canonicalization would widen the attack surface for zero benefit.

New predicate exported via module.exports for direct CJS unit testing — follows the existing escHtml/parsePort pattern in scripts/keel-dashboard.cjs; no new files, honoring the single-file change surface.

Disallowed-Host E2E coverage assigned to node http.request tests, not Playwright — browsers cannot set a spoofed Host header; existing 10 Playwright tests remain the unchanged-behavior regression check.
Did not decide AC-3 status code, port-strictness, or 403 body text — brief assigns these to the architect; recorded as OQ-1..OQ-3 with default recommendations rather than inventing business rules.

Corrected regression baseline from the brief's stale figures (45 tests, 1 known KEEL-103 failure) to the verified reality (46 tests, 46/46 passing) per handshake-gate finding — downstream phases inherit the verified baseline.

decisions: D-1: Missing Host header returns 400 Bad Request (static 'Bad Request' body) — RFC 9112 s3.2 conformance and layer-consistency with Node's own parser; 403 reserved for present-but-disallowed Host (closes OQ-1, defines AC-3).

D-2: Validation is a single pure exported predicate isAllowedHost using one anchored case-insensitive regex over the literal allowlist {localhost, 127.0.0.1, [::1]} with optional 1-5-digit port; any numeric port accepted (brief-literal); no DNS, no canonicalization; fail-closed on every malformed form (closes OQ-2).

D-3: Guard placed as the first handler statements before all routing including 404; rejections via shared rejectRequest helper sending text/plain charset=utf-8 + nosniff + no-store with constant bodies 'Forbidden'/'Bad Request' and zero request echo; renderer structurally unreachable on rejection (closes OQ-3).
D-4: Adopted UI determination as recorded decision — static plain-text rejection is the sole UI surface; no renderer, CSS, or mockup changes.
D-5: Single-file change surface — extend scripts/keel-dashboard.cjs per its existing exported-helper pattern; keel-state.cjs byte-untouched and bin/keel.js unchanged (structural AC-4 guarantee).
D-6: require-time main() execution recorded as design debt with a recommended follow-up chore (require.main guard) — explicitly NOT changed this story to protect the frozen 70-test AC-5 baseline.

D-7: AC-3 E2E coverage must use raw socket / HTTP/1.0 (Node parser pre-rejects Host-less HTTP/1.1); disallowed-Host E2E stays in node http.request tests, Playwright suite remains the unchanged-behavior check (BA assignment adopted).

decisions: Red-check method: in-memory-equivalent mutant injection (KEEL_DASHBOARD_MUTANT env hook + 6 tmpdir mutants) instead of Option A on-disk stubbing — the frozen AC-5 baseline and phase-5 implementation must stay byte-identical on disk, and the hook doubles as a permanent mutation-testing harness scoped strictly to the K105 sections.

AC-3 e2e uses a raw-socket HTTP/1.0 request line, NOT http.request or Playwright, per design D-7: Node's HTTP/1.1 parser rejects Host-less HTTP/1.1 with its own 400 before the handler runs, so only the 1.0/raw path exercises the handler's 400 branch.

Six targeted mutants instead of one blanket stub: header-contract, no-echo, loopback-bind, and export tests all pass under a simple guard-removal stub (the 200 path shares nosniff/no-store), so each failure mode got its own mutant (C/D/E/F) to eliminate every trivially-passing test.

Unit handler tests capture the real request handler via an http.createServer stub and dispatch fake req/res doubles synchronously — external deps (network, port binding) mocked; the guard, predicate, and rejectRequest behavior under test never mocked.

New e2e tests placed in a separate file (tests/keel-dashboard-host-e2e.test.cjs) rather than the unit file: they spawn a real server process and must not slow or destabilize the frozen unit baseline; naming mirrors the keel-state-describe/-e2e split convention.

AC-7 and AC-8 are NOT descoped — they are out-of-scope-for-this-phase with named downstream owners per the approved brief and phase-5 handoff: AC-7 (README security posture + CHANGELOG) owned by phase 11 technical-writer; AC-8 (security patch version proposal, e.g. 3.14.1, with full G-6 stamp checklist) owned by phase 12 release-manager. AC-5 full-suite judgment (incl. Playwright, phase 9) is owned by phase 7 tdd-green onward; this phase executed all four node suites green as evidence, not judgment.

decisions: Did NOT write Playwright tests for disallowed/missing Host: browsers cannot emit a spoofed or absent Host header, so a Playwright test would have to bypass the browser (request context header override), duplicating tests/keel-dashboard-host-e2e.test.cjs without adding browser-level confidence — per BA decision the raw-socket suite owns that coverage.

Classified the 3 initial KEEL-104 spec failures as test-data rot (hard-coded repo snapshot), not regressions, after verifying via manifests that the dashboard output was correct for current state; fixed by deriving expectations from live manifests and pinning only frozen facts.

Gate attempt 1 correctly flagged that the first version of the rot fix weakened the badge assertions (amber IN PROGRESS and red HALTED colors were no longer asserted anywhere) and carried a false coverage comment. Fixed per the gate prescription (text-derived color mapping over all rendered badges + corrected comment) and additionally added a deterministic 3-state fixture test so no badge color can silently lose coverage as repo state evolves.
Ran the KEEL-105 spec against the real repo state (not a tmp fixture) so the browser assertion covers the production-shaped page including the in-flight KEEL-105 row itself.
Used port 7894 (KEEL_E2E_HOST_PORT overridable) to avoid the KEEL-104 spec's 7891-7893 range and the 7772 default, allowing both specs to run concurrently in one Playwright session.

Executed the full 13-test run twice (second run also captured to evidence file) to demonstrate the green result is deterministic, since the first tee attempt failed on a not-yet-created evidence directory.

AC-7 (README security posture + CHANGELOG) and AC-8 (version proposal + G-6 stamp checklist) carried forward with named downstream owners — AC-7 -> phase 11 technical-writer, AC-8 -> phase 12 release-manager — out-of-scope-for-this-phase per the approved brief's phase ownership, not descoped (G-5).

decisions: Consumed prescan.json instead of re-running scanners: npm-audit ran clean and every skip is 'not applicable' or 'not configured' — none FAILED, so re-execution would burn tokens without adding assurance (static-first directive).

Classified the 404-branch header gap LOW/non-blocking rather than fixing it: the branch is pre-existing KEEL-104 code explicitly frozen byte-unchanged by AC-4; changing it here would violate the approved invariant.
Accepted [::1] in the allowlist despite the IPv4-only bind: unreachable over IPv6 by construction, harmless as a Host literal, and spec-complete per the approved brief (AC-1).
Ruled the AC-3 400-vs-403 split not an information-disclosure oracle: it distinguishes malformed from refused per RFC 9112 and discloses nothing the client did not send.
Used repo grep for blast radius after finding codegraph.json stale (no JS nodes); recorded as INFO so the graph tooling gets refreshed rather than silently trusted.

decisions: Documented --port syntax per surface instead of the briefed blanket space-form rule: empirical tests (four live invocations) showed the CLI wrapper accepts only the equals form; publishing a space-form CLI example would violate the tested-and-working examples rule.
Used an '[Unreleased]' CHANGELOG heading with an explicit release-manager note rather than pre-stamping 3.14.1 — phase 12 owns the version decision (AC-8, G-6).
Promoted the constant-body rejection contract straight to conventions.md (not lessons.md): it is a stable how-we-write-code rule born from an approved ADR, not an incident.
Skipped lessons.md: no defect/RCA in this story; adding a non-mandated lesson would spend the 30-entry budget without incident value.
Kept the top-of-README feature bullet (L101) and utilities line (L356) using --port=<N>: both show bin/keel.js / slash-command surfaces where the equals form is the working one.
